steps.title: Set up the sine ratio
steps.content: For angle $A$, sine is the opposite side divided by the hypotenuse. The hypotenuse is $24$, and the adjacent leg is $12$, so the opposite side must be found before evaluating the ratio.

steps.title: Find the opposite side
steps.content:
Use the Pythagorean theorem with the leg $12$ and hypotenuse $24$:
$$
12^2+BC^2=24^2
$$
$$
144+BC^2=576
$$
$$
BC^2=432
$$
$$
BC=12\sqrt{3}
$$

steps.title: Evaluate the sine
steps.content: Now substitute the opposite side and hypotenuse into the ratio: $$ \frac{12\sqrt{3}}{24}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $$ That value is about $0.866$. The answer is 0.866.
